instruction: Extract all core computer science concepts from the following lecture JSON. Return only a newline-separated list of unique concepts. Do not explain anything.

text_blocks.text: Easily understood due to standardized and consistent syntax

runs.text: Easily understood due to standardized and consistent syntax

text_blocks.text: Applications: Software that interfaces with the user

runs.text: : Software that interfaces with the user

text_blocks.text: System Software: Operating System (e.g. Windows, Mac OSX, Linux)

runs.text: : Operating System (e.g. Windows, Mac OSX, Linux)

text_blocks.text: Device Drivers: Software to talk to non-computer devices (e.g. printers, webcams, etc.)

runs.text: : Software to talk to non-computer devices (e.g. printers, webcams, etc.)

text_blocks.text: Java is run on a "Virtual Machine."

runs.text: Java is run on a "Virtual Machine."

text_blocks.text: A Virtual Machine (VM) is an emulation of all of the components (hardware or software) necessary to run a certain piece of software.

runs.text: A Virtual Machine (VM) is an emulation of all of the components (hardware or software) necessary to run a certain piece of software.

text_blocks.text: Code and bytecode works independently from the platform (operating system) it was compiled on.

runs.text: works independently from the platform (operating system) it was compiled on.

text_blocks.text: More secure due to runtime isolation.

runs.text: More secure due to runtime isolation.

text_blocks.text: Generally slower runtime (than natively compiled languages like C++).

runs.text: Generally slower runtime (than natively compiled languages like C++).

text_blocks.text: Arguably provides less fine-tuned usage of lower levels of software

runs.text: Arguably provides less fine-tuned usage of lower levels

text_blocks.text: A "Hello World" program is frequently a student's first program of any language.

runs.text: A "Hello World" program is frequently a student's first program of any language.

text_blocks.text: Similar to learning "Hello" greetings in other languages.

runs.text: Similar to learning "Hello" greetings in other languages.

text_blocks.text: Note: all code is CaSe SeNsItIvE
java – running a compiled java file

runs.text: java – running a compiled java file

text_blocks.text: Ends with a semicolon (like a period in English)

runs.text: Ends with a semicolon (like a period in English)

text_blocks.text: System.out.println("My name is 				Nathan Ong! YAAAAYYYY LALALALA 			RANDOM TYPING");

runs.text: What if we want quotation marks in the String?

runs.text: Escape character \ tells the computer that the next character should not be taken literally

text_blocks.text: I'm so lazy, I don't want to type in my name when printing it out all the time.

runs.text: I'm so lazy, I don't want to type in my name when printing it out all the time.

text_blocks.text: Now I can call name wherever I want (not really)!

runs.text: Now I can call name wherever I want (not really)!

input.title: To Declare a Variable:Type name = value;

text_blocks.text: To Declare a Variable:Type name = value;
The name of a variable (and other things) are called identifiers.

runs.text: The name of a variable (and other things) are called

text_blocks.text: There are some rules regarding valid identifiers, and further conventions that you should follow.

runs.text: There are some rules regarding valid identifiers, and further conventions that you should follow.

text_blocks.text: You must have at least one character in the name

runs.text: You must have at least one character in the name

text_blocks.text: You can only use alphanumeric characters (letters and numbers), underscores (_), and dollar signs ($) in the name

runs.text: You can only use alphanumeric characters (letters and numbers), underscores (_), and dollar signs ($) in the name

text_blocks.text: You CANNOT use a number as the first character.

runs.text: You CANNOT use a number as the first character.

text_blocks.text: You CANNOT use reserved Java keywords.

runs.text: You CANNOT use reserved Java keywords.

text_blocks.text: First word starts with a lowercase letter, subsequent words have uppercase first letters

runs.text: First word starts with a lowercase letter, subsequent words have uppercase first letters

text_blocks.text: All words have uppercase first letters

runs.text: All words have uppercase first letters

text_blocks.text: Never use dollar signs or underscores

runs.text: Never use dollar signs or underscores

text_blocks.text: Oh I changed my name…Nathan Ong is no longer Nathan Ong…What am I going to do?

input.title: What can we do with it?

text_blocks.text: What can we do with it?

runs.text: What can we do with it?

text_blocks.text: No, because we re-declared a variable!

runs.text: No, because we re-declared a variable!
